voice_note: Survive the Month voice pack — first-person SA budgeting game lines

lines.game_start: Welcome to Survive the Month. Pick your life, split your pay, and see if you can stay afloat when life hits.
lines.resume: Welcome back. Your budget is still waiting — let's keep going.
lines.payday: It's payday. Wallet loaded. Time to split the money before life spends it for you.
lines.budget_locked: Budget locked. Now the month begins — four weeks, one decision at a time.
lines.goal_pick: Three months in. You've found your feet. Time to aim at something bigger.
lines.goal_dec: December-proof fund locked in. Kill Janworry before it's born.
lines.goal_study: Study fund it is. Cash for a course means a real income bump later.
lines.goal_car: Car goal set. Cash only — no finance, no interest trap.
lines.goal_home: Home deposit selected. The long game starts now.
lines.goal_none: No goal for now. Just keep surviving. That's still a win.
lines.car: Sigh. Are you serious? The engine is smoking. That's another few thousand I don't have.
lines.brakes: The brakes and tyres won't make another month. That's a massive expense.
lines.tyre: A pothole got me. Flat tyre and alignment — there goes my afternoon.
lines.taxi: Another taxi fare hike, and the queues meant extra trips this week.
lines.fridge: Stage six load-shedding defrosted the fridge. All my groceries are spoiled!
lines.plumb: A leaking pipe won't wait. Plumber call-out fees always sting.
lines.appliance: The washing machine gave in. The repair quote is basically a replacement.
lines.petrol: Petrol and food prices jumped again. Everything is getting more expensive.
lines.medical: Oh no — a sudden medical bill. Tests and meds I didn't plan for.
lines.dental: A toothache that won't wait. Dentist bills are never cheap.
lines.family: Family back home needs support. Life is tough, but we stand together.
lines.funeral: A funeral in the family. Everyone is expected to contribute.
lines.wedding: A cousin is getting married, and the family list came around.
lines.school: School uniforms, shoes, and stationery. The ask came to me.
lines.phone: Cracked phone screen! It slipped out of my hand at the worst time.
lines.stolen: My phone was stolen right out of my hand. I need a replacement to work.
lines.geyser: Burst geyser at two a.m. Water through the ceiling — the worst word in home ownership.
lines.rates: Municipal rates and maintenance bills I didn't expect.
lines.hustle: Yes! The side hustle paid off. Extra cash in my pocket!
lines.refund: An overcharge got reversed. A small refund win.
lines.overtime: Weekend overtime pays off. Extra hours, extra cash!
lines.quiet: A quiet week. Nothing dramatic. Enjoy the calm while it lasts.
lines.credit: Swiping the credit card. It's expensive, but I've got no choice.
lines.festive: December hit. Festive travel and gifts home — you knew this bill was coming.
lines.schoolfees: January school fees and uniforms. Plan for it, or it plans for you.
lines.winter: Winter electricity spike. The geyser and heater are eating rands.
lines.licence: Car licence renewal. Another fixed cost you can't postpone.
lines.gift: Thirteenth cheque! Your employer paid a December bonus. Don't let it evaporate.
lines.sars: SARS tax refund! Filing season paid off — they owe you for once.
lines.treat: You've earned this. A weekend away, the outfit, a braai for the crew — your call.
lines.snap: You snapped. Months of all grind and no joy caught up. Retail therapy happened first.
lines.penalty: You cut it too close. Underfunded bills landed with late fees. Saving on paper cost more in real life.
lines.action_save: Banked into the emergency fund. Steady wins.
lines.action_debt: Threw it at debt. Interest will thank you.
lines.action_spend_good: Treated yourself while net worth climbed. That's momentum, not a leak.
lines.action_spend_bad: Treated yourself while things were sliding. Enjoy it — but watch the leak.
lines.action_treat: You took the break. Zero guilt. Rested people hustle better.
lines.action_skip: You skipped the treat. Discipline is good — but all gas and no oil burns the engine.
lines.action_fund: Used the emergency fund. That's what it's for. Top it back up when you can.
lines.action_cut: Cut lifestyle and spare cash. No fun, but no big new debt.
lines.action_goal: Raided the goal fund. The ten percent penalty is the price of breaking a promise to yourself.
lines.action_credit: Put it on credit. Easy now, expensive later. It compounds.
lines.absorb_full: Your buffers covered it entirely. Planning paid off.
lines.car_replace: New wheels, paid cash. No finance. That car debt monster stays outside.
lines.car_sell: Sold the car and switched to taxis. Cash in hand, breakdown risk gone.
lines.car_keep: Keeping the car for now. Just remember — wear only goes one direction.
lines.month_survived: Month survived. Score is in. Breathe — then set up next month stronger.
lines.win: You won. Three-month emergency fund, debt cleared. That's real financial resilience.
lines.lose: Game over. Debt snowballed past what income could fix. A buffer before the storm changes everything.
lines.badge: New badge unlocked. Progress you can keep across every run.
lines.raise: You got a raise! A little more breathing room each month.
lines.cut_hours: Hours were cut. Income down. Time to tighten the plan.
lines.stokvel: Stokvel paid out with a ten percent bonus — just in time for December.
lines.endless: Endless mode. The foundation is built. What's next on the list?
